summary: Excellent Betty Board sound captures a hot late‑May ’77 show with standout performances of Sugaree, Bertha and Tennessee Jed.
review: The Ashley/Bertha “Betty” board delivers crystal‑clear, well‑remastered sound that shines above other May ’77 sources. Musically the concert is consistently above average – a strong Bertha opener, a top‑tier Sugaree, tight Samson & Delilah and an angular yet thrilling Tennessee Jed make it a must‑listen for fans of the spring ’77 peak.

ai_rating.rationale: Both show quality (above‑average with many highlighted songs) and recording quality (excellent soundboard, praised for clarity) are strong; weighted average yields ~4.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Fine recording and remaster; the Betty Board shines, crisp and clear, out‑shining other May ’77 sources that are described as muffled.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A consistently hot performance from start to finish – a strong opener sets the tone and both sets stay above‑average, delivering classic spring ’77 energy without reaching the absolute top‑ten tier.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone is praised; his playing is described as angular on Tennessee Jed and he takes interesting extensions on several songs.
